_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize MI federal-House challenger batch: MI-07 D primary (Badger, Cowen, Rais) + MI-08 R primary (Lemmo, Hassan). Social-media + candidacy-currency protocol. Cowen & Rais WITHDREW -> not_running. Every TRUE/FALSE cited to candidate's own platform/self-ID; null where no evidence; never inferred from party.

set.office: U.S. Representative MI-07 (2026 D Candidate · challenger · Barrett challenger)

profile.background: Michigan Army National Guard veteran; IT contractor/small contractor from Wolverine, MI. Self-described Democratic Socialist and 'LGBTQIA+ activist'; runs a protest-style campaign. First-time candidate; no prior office. Raised Baptist (per Lansing City Pulse).
profile.religion: Raised Baptist (per profile; current affiliation not stated)

elyon-badger.notes_set: Candidacy CURRENT as of 2026-06: FEC statutory candidate H6MI07264 (committee C00909002, Statement of Candidacy 2025-06-27); listed in the 2026-08-04 MI-07 Democratic primary field (Ballotpedia). Active campaign site. Scored from his OWN campaign platform + self-identification only. HARD: self-identified 'LGBTQIA+ activist' (own bio) -> contrary to all conservative marriage/sexuality positions; platform 'Abolish and Imprison ICE' -> contrary to mandatory-deportation. Abortion direction appears pro-choice but only an unverified search snippet was found -> nulled (not scored). The most extreme statements attributed to him circulate only via NRCC with no sourced quotes -> NOT scored. Guns, elections, foreign policy, religious liberty: NO EVIDENCE FOUND -> null.

0.note: Self-identified 'LGBTQIA+ activist' in his own bio -> does not affirm marriage as exclusively one man/one woman.

1.note: Self-identified LGBTQIA+ activist -> supports same-sex marriage, contrary to opposing it in law.

2.note: Self-identified LGBTQIA+ activist -> affirms transgender identity, does not reject gender ideology.

4.note: Runs as an LGBTQIA+ activist -> affirmatively promotes LGBTQ+ identity in public policy.

1.note: Campaign platform: 'Abolish and Imprison ICE' -> opposes the deportation/enforcement apparatus, contrary to mandatory deportation.

set.office: U.S. Representative MI-07 (withdrew 2025 · former 2026 D candidate · Barrett)

profile.background: Professor of education policy at Michigan State University; leading national critic of private-school vouchers/choice (author, 'The Privateers', Harvard Education Press 2024). First-time candidate; no prior office.

josh-cowen.notes_set: WITHDREW from the MI-07 race on 2025-10-08 (suspended campaign, citing inability to compete financially) and endorsed Bridget Brink -> candidacy_status not_running. Not on the 2026 ballot. Only HARD documented position: career-defining OPPOSITION to school vouchers/private-school choice (book 'The Privateers', NEPC, MEA; NEA 2025 Friend of Education) -> family_child_sovereignty q0 FALSE. All other topics: NO EVIDENCE FOUND -> null. Status-fix is the substantive update here.

0.note: Career-defining opponent of school vouchers/private-school choice (book 'The Privateers'; campaign centered on protecting public schools) -> contrary to supporting universal school choice.

set.office: U.S. Representative MI-07 (withdrew · former 2026 D candidate · Barrett)

profile.background: Family/internal medicine physician (MD) in Dewitt, MI. Prior unsuccessful runs: lost 2020 MI House general, lost 2022 MI Senate primary. No office held.

muhammad-salman-rais.notes_set: WITHDREW from MI-07: Michigan's OFFICIAL candidate list dated 2026-06-05 lists him as 'Withdrew' for U.S. House MI-07; FEC committee C00902593 reported $0.00 receipts/disbursements (never funded a campaign) -> candidacy_status not_running. Researched via social-media protocol: NO policy footprint found on any of the 10 federal rubric topics (no campaign Issues page, Vote411 questionnaire, or issue-group endorsements). Only vague non-position slogans -> all cells null. Confidence set evidence_federal to record that research was done and nothing scoreable was found.

set.office: U.S. Representative MI-08 (2026 R Candidate · challenger · McDonald Rivet challenger)

profile.background: Retired mechanical engineer (30+ yrs U.S. Army TARDEC, Warren MI); Catholic active in parish St. Vincent de Paul Society and a Wayne County Jail prison ministry. Longtime Right to Life of metro Detroit board member; ran for MI-13 in 2020 (lost GOP primary). Republican precinct delegate ~25 yrs.

alfred-lemmo.notes_set: Candidacy CURRENT: active R candidate in the 2026-08-04 MI-08 primary vs. Amir Hassan (Ballotpedia; live campaign site). Scored from campaign Issues page + biography. HARD pro-life: decades on Right to Life board, Issues page opposes tax-funded abortion, supports born-alive protections and pregnancy resource centers -> sanctity_of_life q0 TRUE; Issues page 'Oppose assisted suicide' -> q3 TRUE. Note: uses INCREMENTAL 'reasonable national limits on late-term abortions', not abolition -> abolition cell q1 left null (no abolition advocacy). Religious liberty: Issues page protects conscience rights for doctors/nurses/faith-based hospitals + supports faith-based adoption agencies -> christian_liberty q1 TRUE and family_child_sovereignty q4 TRUE. Economy (conventional GOP tax/dereg), marriage/LGBTQ, border, guns, elections, foreign policy: NO EVIDENCE on the specific rubric cells -> null. PP/EMILY's List funding history not verifiable -> q4 null.

0.note: Decades on Right to Life board; Issues page opposes federal funding of abortion providers and supports protections for babies born alive -> affirms sanctity/personhood of unborn life.

1.note: Issues page: 'Protect conscience rights for Michigan doctors, nurses, and faith-based hospitals'; supports faith-based adoption agencies.

4.note: Issues page supports faith-based adoption agencies (conscience protection for faith-based placement).

set.office: U.S. Representative MI-08 (2026 R Candidate · challenger · McDonald Rivet challenger)

profile.background: Flint native, U.S. Navy veteran and former federal law-enforcement officer (watch commander at Guantanamo Bay). Trump-aligned 'America First' first-time candidate; endorsed by Reps. Bergman, Huizenga, and James (2026-02). Signed U.S. Term Limits pledge.
profile.religion: Not stated (respects all faiths; personal religion not publicly confirmed)

amir-hassan-mi-08.notes_set: Candidacy CURRENT: active R candidate in the 2026-08-04 MI-08 primary vs. Alfred Lemmo (Ballotpedia; FEC C00675256; campaign news page 2026-02-04). Scored from campaign Issues content. HARD transgender stance: 'radical gender ideology of boys becoming girls, using girls' bathrooms, and playing girls' sports has got to stop' -> biblical_marriage q2 TRUE and q4 TRUE. HARD border: 'wholeheartedly supports President Trump's efforts to deport illegal immigrants, close the border' -> border_immigration q0 TRUE and q1 TRUE. CROSS-PRESSURE (deviation): self-described 'pro-life' but explicitly PRO-IVF (daughter conceived via IVF) and supports rape/incest/life exceptions -> contrary to opposing IVF embryo discard and to personhood-from-conception, so sanctity_of_life q2 FALSE; q0/q1 left null (does not meet the personhood/abolition bar). Same-sex marriage, elections, guns specifics, religious liberty, economy specifics (CBDC/Fed/budget/ESG), foreign-policy-restraint cells: NO EVIDENCE on those specific cells -> null (his pro-Israel/anti-Iran posture does not map onto the restraint rubric).

2.note: Explicitly pro-IVF (daughter conceived via IVF) -> does not oppose IVF embryo discard.
'The radical gender ideology of boys becoming girls, using girls bathrooms, and playing girls sports has got to stop' -> rejects transgender ideology, affirms biological sex.

4.note: Opposes gender ideology in girls' school sports and bathrooms -> opposes promotion of LGBTQ+/gender identity in schools.

0.note: 'Wholeheartedly supports President Trump's efforts to ... close the border' -> supports a secured physical border.

1.note: 'Wholeheartedly supports President Trump's efforts to deport illegal immigrants' -> supports mandatory deportation.
